title: Tangential ambient operators
summary: Formalizes equality modulo Q and weighted tangentiality, packages the formal GJMS candidate, and records that the critical tangentiality theorem is still planned in this module.

mathematicalContent.heading: Boundary values modulo Q
mathematicalContent.body: Because the cone is represented by Q = 0, two ambient extensions have the same boundary value when their difference is divisible by Q. At a fixed input weight w, the quotient h in f − g = Qh is required to have weight w − 2.
mathematicalContent.formula: f\sim_Q g\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad f-g=Qh;\qquad f\sim_{Q,w}g\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad f-g=Qh,\ Xh=(w-2)h.

mathematicalContent.heading: Weighted tangentiality
mathematicalContent.body: An ambient operator P is tangential at weight w if its boundary value is independent of the chosen weight-w extension. For the GJMS construction the expected operator is the k-th ambient Laplacian at w = k − n/2, with output weight −k − n/2.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formalization boundary
mathematicalContent.body: The module proves the equivalence-relation algebra and weight bookkeeping under AlgebraicIdentities. ambientGJMSCandidate still takes tangentiality as an input hypothesis. The theorem asserting tangentiality of the k-th Laplacian is shown only inside a documentation block as future work and is not compiled here.
mathematicalContent.formula: \operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeight}\!\left(\widetilde\Delta^k,k-\frac n2\right)\quad\text{is not proved in this file}.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Two ambient functions have the same boundary value when they are congruent modulo Q, equivalently when their difference is a Q-multiple.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: f\sim_Q g\quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad f\equiv g\pmod Q\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad\exists h,\ f-g=Qh.
correspondence.leanType: def SameBoundaryValue (f g : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Weighted boundary equivalence records both f − g = Qh and the expected shifted homogeneity Xh = (w − 2)h of the quotient.
correspondence.leanType: def SameBoundaryValueAtWeight (w : ℝ) (f g : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: SameBoundaryValue is exactly the previously defined congruence EqModQ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_def (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g ↔ CalConf.EqModQ f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Weighted boundary equivalence implies ordinary boundary equivalence after forgetting the homogeneity condition on h.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValueAtWeight.sameBoundaryValue {w : ℝ} {f g : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValueAtWeight w f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Every explicit Q-multiple vanishes modulo Q.
correspondence.leanType: theorem VanishesModQ_Qmul (h : Function Conf) : CalConf.VanishesModQ (CalConf.Qmul h)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zero function vanishes modulo Q, with quotient h = 0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem VanishesModQ_zero : CalConf.VanishesModQ (zero Conf)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Congruence modulo Q is equivalent to the existence of a global quotient h for the difference f − g.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: f\equiv g\pmod Q\iff\exists h\in\mathcal F(\widetilde{\mathcal G}),\ f-g=Qh.
correspondence.leanType: theorem EqModQ_iff_exists_Qmul (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.EqModQ f g ↔ ∃ h : Function Conf, (fun U => f U - g U) = CalConf.Qmul h

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary equivalence is reflexive.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_refl (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary equivalence is symmetric; if f − g = Qh then g − f = Q(−h).
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_symm {f g : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary equivalence is transitive; Q-divisible differences add.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_trans {f g k : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g) (hgk : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g k) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Literal equality of ambient functions implies equality of their boundary values.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_of_eq {f g : Function Conf} (h : f = g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary equivalence is compatible with pointwise addition.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_add {f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f₁ f₂) (hg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g₁ g₂) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (add Conf f₁ g₁) (add Conf f₂ g₂)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary equivalence is compatible with pointwise subtraction.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_sub {f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f₁ f₂) (hg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g₁ g₂) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (sub Conf f₁ g₁) (sub Conf f₂ g₂)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary equivalence is preserved by multiplication by any real constant.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_smul (c : ℝ) {f g : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (smul Conf c f) (smul Conf c g)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An ambient scalar operator is any map from ambient functions to ambient functions; linearity is not included in this abbreviation.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev Operator : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An operator is tangential when its output modulo Q depends only on its input modulo Q.
correspondence.leanType: def IsTangentialOperator (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This weaker predicate requires P to send every Q-divisible function to another Q-divisible function. For a general nonlinear P it is not equivalent to tangentiality.
correspondence.leanType: def PreservesVanishesModQ (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The identity operator preserves boundary equivalence and is therefore tangential.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsTangentialOperator.id : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator (fun f : Function Conf => f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The composition of two tangential operators is tangential.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsTangentialOperator.comp {P R : Operator (Conf := Conf)} (hP : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator P) (hR : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator R) : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator (fun f => P (R f))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: P is tangential at weight w if any two weight-w extensions whose difference is Qh with h of weight w − 2 produce outputs with the same boundary value.
correspondence.leanType: def IsTangentialAtWeight (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) (w : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: P maps weight w₁ to weight w₂ when every X-homogeneous input of weight w₁ is sent to an X-homogeneous output of weight w₂.
correspondence.leanType: def MapsHomogeneousWeight (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This structure packages an input weight, an output weight, an ambient operator P, a proof of its weight mapping, and a proof of tangentiality at the input weight.
correspondence.leanType: structure WeightedTangentialOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The critical input weight for the k-th ambient GJMS construction is k − n/2.
correspondence.leanType: def criticalWeight (k : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Applying k Laplacians lowers the critical input weight by 2k, giving output weight −k − n/2.
correspondence.leanType: def gjmsOutputWeight (k : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under AlgebraicIdentities, the k-th ambient Laplacian sends an X-homogeneous function of critical weight k − n/2 to one of weight −k − n/2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_has_GJMS_weight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (CalConf.criticalWeight k) f) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (CalConf.gjmsOutputWeight k) (CalConf.lapPow k f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Given AlgebraicIdentities and a separate proof that the k-th ambient Laplacian is tangential at the critical weight, this constructor packages Δ̃^k as a weighted tangential operator.
correspondence.leanType: def ambientGJMSCandidate (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (fun f : Function Conf => CalConf.lapPow k f) (CalConf.criticalWeight k)) : CalConf.WeightedTangentialOperator

references.locator: Chapter 3, ambient construction of conformally invariant powers of the Laplacian
references.note: Geometric source for extension independence, critical weights, and the ambient GJMS construction.

limitations: The equivalence-relation and weight bookkeeping results are Lean-verified, with the latter depending on AlgebraicIdentities. The crucial theorem that Δ̃^k is tangential at the critical weight is not implemented in this module: it appears only in a documentation comment, and ambientGJMSCandidate therefore requires tangentiality as an explicit input.
